name: french rolls
instructions: 1.5 tsp salt, 3 cups all-purpose flour, 0.5 cup wheat flour, 1.5 tbsp yeast. Bake at 425°F.
notes:
Adapted from Julia Child (Mastering the Art of French Cooking).

ADAPTED RATIOS (THE CURRENT RECIPE): the version above is what I landed on after iterating on Julia's original. Tighter salt, more yeast, and a different flour split work much better for me than her published version.

JULIA CHILD'S ORIGINAL: her original called for 2 tsp salt, 3.5 cups all-purpose flour, 2 tbsp wheat flour, and 1 tbsp yeast. I found that version came out too salty, too dense, and too plain. The adjustments I made:
- DROP SALT from 2 tsp → 1.5 tsp (fixes the too-salty problem)
- BUMP YEAST from 1 tbsp → 1.5 tbsp (more rise, less dense)
- REWORK THE FLOUR SPLIT: 3 cups AP + 0.5 cup wheat flour (instead of 3.5 cups AP + 2 tbsp wheat) — more wheat flour gives the rolls more character without taking over.

If you want to taste the original alongside the adapted version sometime, run a side-by-side to confirm the changes are real. My memory of the original is that the differences are noticeable.
